headline.purpose: Section headers across marketing and product pages.

body.purpose: Running copy, form help text, descriptions.

label.purpose: Eyebrows, breadcrumb segments, stat captions, badges.

shadows.name: glow-border
shadows.purpose: Azure-tinted ambient glow for outline-glow buttons and glow-enabled cards.

shadows.name: modal-shadow
shadows.purpose: Lift modal panels off the page; the only surface with a real drop shadow.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Default entrance/state-transition easing across the whole app.

motion.name: duration-entrance
motion.purpose: Modal panels, page entrances, fade/scale/slide-in utilities.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main call-to-action across wizard steps and review page.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #3D6FA8; color: #FDF8F0; font-weight: 600; font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; height: 2.75rem; padding: 0 1.5rem; border-radius: 9999px; border: none; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; transition: background 0.15s, transform 0.15s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #4A82C4; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #3D6FA8; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Secondary action, transparent with hairline border.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: transparent; color: #1C1710; font-weight: 600; font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; height: 2.75rem; padding: 0 1.5rem; border-radius: 9999px; border: 1px solid rgba(90,65,30,0.12); transition: background 0.15s, border-color 0.15s; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #E4DCCB; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #3D6FA8; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Default surface container for templates, review sections, stat panels.
components.html:
Logic App

HTTP-triggered workflow with a pre-wired Azure OpenAI action.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #EDE6D6; border: 1px solid rgba(90,65,30,0.12); border-radius: 0.75rem; padding: 1.5rem; transition: background 0.15s, border-color 0.15s; } .ds-card:hover { background: #E4DCCB; border-color: rgba(90,65,30,0.24); } .ds-card-title { font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; color: #1C1710; margin: 0 0 0.5rem; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 0.875rem; color: rgba(55,42,22,0.62); margin: 0; }

components.name: Default Badge
components.description: Status/category tag used on template rows and review summaries.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-block; background: #E8E1D0; color: #1C1710; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; padding: 0.125rem 0.625rem; border-radius: 9999px; border: 1px solid rgba(90,65,30,0.12); }

components.name: Modal
components.description: Proof-artifact confirmation modal (ConfirmModal) — the only shadowed surface in the system.
components.html:
Deployment submitted
Proof artifact text goes here.
components.css: .ds-modal-overlay { background: rgba(0,0,0,0.6); position: fixed; inset: 0; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; } .ds-modal { background: #E4DCCB; border: 1px solid rgba(90,65,30,0.40); border-radius: 1rem; box-shadow: 0 25px 50px -12px rgba(0,0,0,0.25), 0 0 0 1px rgba(61,111,168,0.12), 0 0 24px rgba(61,111,168,0.04); max-width: clamp(32rem, 70vw, 56rem); width: 100%; } .ds-modal-header { font-size: 1.125rem; font-weight: 600; color: #1C1710; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(90,65,30,0.12); padding: 0.75rem 1.5rem; } .ds-modal-body { padding: 1rem 1.5rem 1.25rem; color: #1C1710; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.875rem; }

narrative.overview: A deployment tool that reads like a warm paper form on an engineer's desk, not a SaaS dashboard. The canvas is cream, the ink is charcoal, and the one moment of color, a dusty muted azure, is reserved for the thing the user is actually about to do: submit, confirm, deploy. One deliberate exception: TerminalHero and AsciiTerminal drop into a dark amber CRT palette inside their own bounded card, evoking an old terminal session. It's an isolated motif, not a second theme.
keyCharacteristics: Warm, tinted-cream neutrals — never pure white or pure black
Single accent (dusty azure) carries interactive/primary meaning; nothing else competes for it
Flat surfaces, tonal layering instead of shadows
Mono type reserved for machine-generated or terminal-flavored strings only
One contained dark/amber motif, never bleeding into the rest of the page

rules.name: The One Ink Rule
rules.body: Dusty azure is the only color allowed to signal "primary action." Sage, amber, and semantic colors never substitute for it.

rules.name: The Warm Neutral Rule
rules.body: No neutral in this system is gray. A pure #fff/#000/gray-scale value anywhere is a bug.

rules.name: The Mono-Means-Machine Rule
rules.body: IBM Plex Mono only ever wraps something the system generated or a structural eyebrow label, never a sentence a human wrote.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Cards, badges, and inputs never cast a shadow. Promote a tonal layer before reaching for box-shadow.

dos: Do keep every neutral tinted warm — cream backgrounds, sepia borders, charcoal text.
Do reserve dusty azure (#3D6FA8) for primary actions, links, and focus rings only.
Do keep motion nearly invisible: 150ms state transitions, 350-420ms entrances, ease-out-expo, no bounce.
Do keep form labels always visible, never placeholder-only.
Do treat the terminal amber motif as a contained illustration, bounded to its own card.
donts: Don't introduce consumer-SaaS patterns: gradients, hero-metric tiles, feature-marketing copy, mascots.
Don't build Azure Portal-style heavy UI: stacked panels, dense chrome, nested toolbars.
Don't reach for neon or cyberpunk saturation anywhere outside the bounded terminal motif.
Don't over-animate: no choreographed entrances, no scroll-driven sequences, no decorative looping motion outside status indicators.
Don't use border-left/border-right as a colored accent stripe anywhere.
Don't let the terminal amber palette leak into any component outside TerminalHero/AsciiTerminal.
Don't use pure #000/#fff or untinted gray.
